title: Smoky Juicy Croatian Sausage Roast
description: A hearty, smoky lamb roast paired with savory Croatian sausages, simmered in a fragrant blend of herbs and wine for a perfect kosher-friendly centerpiece.
instructions: Preheat oven to 160 °C (325 °F). Pat the lamb dry and rub it with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, and rosemary. Let it rest for 15 min.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat. Brown the lamb on all sides, about 3–4 min per side, then set aside.
In the same pot, add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 3–4 min. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Return the lamb to the pot, arrange the sausages around it, and pour in the white wine, broth, and tomato paste. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Roast for 90 min or until the lamb reaches an internal temperature of 70 °C (160 °F).
Baste the lamb and sausages every 20 min with the pan juices to keep them moist.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the lamb rest for 10 min before slicing.
Serve the sliced lamb and sausages over the roasted onions, spooning pan juices over the top.
Optional: Garnish with extra rosemary sprigs for added aroma.
Pair the dish with crusty bread or boiled potatoes for a complete meal.
